title: Define Zephyr Kconfig and devicetree model for HAL capabilities
description: Why this exists: partner board ports should be mostly overlays, Kconfig fragments, and driver bindings, not application rewrites. We need a stable way to express board capabilities and feature gates. What needs to be done: define Kconfig symbols and devicetree conventions for LICHEN board capabilities: CONFIG_LICHEN_HAS_DISPLAY, HAS_BUTTONS, HAS_LEDS, HAS_GNSS, HAS_BATTERY, HAS_PMIC, HAS_BLE_LOCAL, HAS_SERIAL_LOCAL, HAS_EXTERNAL_FLASH, radio model selection, UI profile, and source-provider support for location/time. Ensure runtime capability discovery can be derived from build-time config where appropriate. Acceptance criteria: Kconfig options and devicetree aliases/chosen nodes are documented; sample overlays exist for reference boards or fake/native test targets; invalid combinations fail at build time where possible; headless builds do not pull display/UI code; two code-review passes are completed before closure.
notes:
Progress from project-LICHEN-ev5b.3: added CONFIG_LICHEN_HAS_* capability symbols, radio model choices with compatible validation, UI/location/time provider choices, serial chosen conventions, GPIO LED/button alias validation, and native_sim default/loopback HAL tests. Follow-up project-LICHEN-6mco tracks missing positive serial-local precedence test. Keeping h7t5.3 open because broader reference board overlays/docs and additional invalid-combination test fixtures remain.
Completed remaining no-hardware Kconfig/devicetree model validation slice: added scripts/zephyr-hal-negative-builds.sh to exercise HAL invalid-combination BUILD_ASSERT coverage where Twister has no first-class expected-build-failure field in this Zephyr 3.7 tree. The helper proves bad configs fail with expected diagnostics for: CONFIG_LICHEN_HAS_LORA without chosen zephyr,lora; CONFIG_LICHEN_HAS_BATTERY without battery0; and LICHEN_RADIO_MODEL_SX126X selected against a loopback lora chosen node. This complements existing positive/absent HAL Twister coverage for native defaults, LoRa loopback, power providers, reset/reboot/HWINFO, serial precedence, and native serial init.

Validation evidence: local git diff --check passed. EC2 us-east-2a builder i-0875d1ad33af5db66 with EBS vol-0875d0df8bdf82a58 ran OUT_DIR=$PWD/build/hal-negative ./scripts/zephyr-hal-negative-builds.sh from /mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN-h7t5-3. Result: PASS lora-missing-chosen; PASS battery-missing-alias; PASS radio-model-mismatch; HAL negative build checks passed. Then ran west twister -T lichen/tests/hal -T lichen/tests/hal_serial_precedence -T lichen/tests/hal_reset_unavailable -p native_sim -p qemu_x86 --inline-logs --outdir twister-out-h7t5-3-hal --extra-args=ZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES=$PWD/lichen. Result: 14 scenarios / 28 instances selected, 21/28 configurations passed, 7 static skips, 0 failed, 0 errored, 0 warnings, 331 test cases executed, 113 skipped, duration 292.56s.

Explorer evidence: current model centralizes CONFIG_LICHEN_HAS_* and provider choices in lichen/subsys/lichen/hal/Kconfig, enforces devicetree contracts with BUILD_ASSERTs in hal.c, covers positive fake/native LoRa/power/serial/reset paths in lichen/tests/hal and hal_serial_precedence, and documents board capability state in docs/firmware-hal-contract.md plus docs/firmware-board-capability-matrix.md. Remaining positive fake fixtures for display/buttons/LEDs/external flash/BLE are useful future broadening but not required to close this no-hardware model feature because unsupported behavior and Kconfig/devicetree contracts are already explicit; hardware/long-tail board validation remains outside this Bead.
Final code-review evidence for validation helper slice: three independent reviews ran after staging scripts/zephyr-hal-negative-builds.sh and h7t5.3 evidence. Agents 019f25f6-31f6-7bc2-be42-c57d5c429073 and 019f25f6-8ab2-7a53-be03-951417f06f4e reported no P0/P1/P2 findings; both checked script syntax and staged diff. Agent 019f25f6-a097-71a1-bde1-41986c7c2b78 reported one procedural P2 that review outcomes were not recorded in Beads before closure; this note resolves that evidence gap. Unrelated lichen/boards/renode/nucleo_wl55jc/_integration_test.resc remains untracked and unstaged; no build/ or twister-out output is staged.
